channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Cousins and Falcons do just enough to beat Raiders 15-9 and keep pace with Bucs in NFC South
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Ahead most of the game, including by double digits in the fourth quarter, the Atlanta Falcons were just trying to hold on at the end. The Raiders had two passes into the end zone. Two passes to steal a victory and hand Atlanta a considerable setback to its playoff hopes.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Kirk Cousins acknowledges he must play better if Atlanta Falcons are to make a playoff push
item.description: If the Atlanta Falcons are going to make a playoff push, coach Raheem Morris and quarterback Kirk Cousins both know the 13-year veteran needs to improve his play over the last three games of the season.
item.dc:creator: W.G. Ramirez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Florida county poised to vote on financing for new $1.3B ballpark for the Rays
item.description: After two costly delays, the Pinellas County Commission is set to vote on its share of financing for a new $1.3 billion Tampa Bay Rays ballpark.
item.dc:creator: Kate Payne And Curt Anderson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Falcons snap 4-game losing streak in much-needed win over Raiders
item.description: The Atlanta Falcons snapped their 4-game losing streak, as Kirk Cousins finally threw a touchdown pass in a win over the Las Vegas Raiders on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vikings win 7th straight to forge tie for NFC North lead with 30-12 blowout of reeling Bears
item.description: Aaron Jones and Cam Akers had touchdown runs in the second half to complement a smothering performance by the Minnesota defense, and the Vikings moved into a tie for first place in the NFC North by beating the Chicago Bears 30-12 for their seventh consecutive victory.
item.dc:creator: Dave Campbell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: BYU excited to face Heisman winner Travis Hunter
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - As BYU continues preparation for the Alamo Bowl December 28th against Colorado, they are looking forward to facing off against Heisman Trophy Winner Travis Hunter. Hunter is truly a one-of-a-kind player.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: The 49ers have suspended De'Vondre Campbell for final 3 games
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — The San Francisco 49ers suspended linebacker De’Vondre Campbell for the rest of the regular season after he refused to enter a game after losing his starting job.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: SJSU’s Nick Nash makes All-America team highlighted by Heisman winner Hunter, unanimous pick Jeanty
item.description: Nash led the country in receptions (104), receiving yards (1,382) and receiving touchdowns (16), becoming the fourth player ever to achieve the receiving triple crown.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: David Montgomery to get season-ending knee surgery as Lions' injury woes continue to stack
item.description: David Montgomery is lost for the remainder of the 2024 season, as Detroit Lions head coach Dan Campbell announced he will get surgery to repair a torn MCL from Sunday's loss.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Detroit Lions lose Montgomery, McNeill, Dorsey for the season as injuries sap Super Bowl hopes
item.description: ALLEN PARK, Mich. (AP) — The banged-up Detroit Lions took some serious hits in their loss to the Buffalo Bills. “Nobody cares,” coach Dan Campbell said Monday. “Nobody is going to give us a pass, or put an asterisk next to your record.
item.dc:creator: LARRY LAGE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: LeBron James returns to score 18 in Lakers' win vs Grizzlies after missing 2 games with foot injury
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — LeBron James returned to the Los Angeles Lakers on Sunday night in a 116-110 victory over the Memphis Grizzlies after a two-game break. James is fighting a left foot injury and was away from the team most of last week because of personal reasons.
item.dc:creator: KYLE GLASER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Caitlin Clark's brother seemingly responds to Mystics owner's slight of sister's Time cover
item.description: Caitlin Clark's brother, Colin, appeared to respond to Washington Mystics owner Sheila Johnson's recent comments about his sister's Time magazine cover for Athlete of the Year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Saquon Barkley slowed by 'minor' injury, loses ground in pursuit of Dickerson's record
item.description: Eagles running back Saquon Barkley had a setback in his chase of the NFL season rushing record when he appeared slowed by an undisclosed injury.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Josh Allen dazzles in Bills’ win over Lions in high-scoring bout
item.description: The Buffalo Bills took down the Detroit Lions on the road Sunday in a 48-42 thriller that saw Josh Allen add to his MVP resume with four total touchdowns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mayfield throws for 4 TDs, including 2 to Evans, as Bucs dominate second half to rout Chargers 40-17
item.description: Baker Mayfield threw for 288 yards and four touchdown passes, Mike Evans had a season-high 159 receiving yards and two scores and the Tampa Bay Buccaneers dominated the second half en route to a 40-17 victory over the Los Angeles Chargers on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY AP Sports Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bill Belichick's former players speak out after coach's shocking decision to take North Carolina job
item.description: Super Bowl champions Tedy Bruschi and Julian Edelman weigh in on Bill Belichick's shocking decision to leave the NFL ranks for the North Carolina Tar Heels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mahomes throws 2 TD passes, leaves with ankle injury in Chiefs' 21-7 win. X-rays negative on star QB
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — The only thing the Chiefs had to worry about in the final minutes Sunday was Patrick Mahomes' ankle.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Miami Dolphins wide receiver Grant DuBose taken off the field on a stretcher after hit to the head
item.description: Grant DuBose, who returned Sunday after a shoulder injury sidelined him for part of the season, was listed as being in stable condition.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 3 Utah cowboys earn world champion titles at 2024 National Finals Rodeo
item.description: Three cowboys from Utah won world champion titles at the 2024 Wrangler National Finals Rodeo in Las Vegas on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, ABC 4

item.title: Freddie Freeman's walk-off grand slam ball sells for $1.56 million at auction
item.description: Freddie Freeman’s historic walk-off grand slam ball from Game 1 of the World Series has sold for $1.56 million at auction.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Utah runs road winning streak to six straight with 4-3 victory
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Hockey Club has become true road warriors. Clayton Keller scored a power-play goal with 43.8 seconds remaining and the Utah Hockey Club rallied to beat the San Jose Sharks 4-3 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Trump gets warm welcome as he watches Army-Navy game alongside key allies, nominees and Daniel Penny
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump huddled with allies and a Republican cause célèbre at Saturday's Army-Navy football game, taking in one of the most storied rivalries in college sports while spotlighting his emerging national security team.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, TARA COPP and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jazz come up short in shootout with Phoenix, 134-126
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - In an incredible display of shooting by both teams, the Utah Jazz could not keep up with the Phoenix Suns in a 134-126 loss Friday night at the Delta Center. Both teams made 22 three-pointers and shot well over 50 percent from the field.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Bucs' run game faced with challenging front seven
item.description: Quarterback Baker Mayfield is faced with a challenging Chargers defense that is allowing the fewest points in the NFL. The Chargers are led by the edge-rush combo of Joey Bosa and Khalil Mack.
item.dc:creator: Aileen Hnatiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: With Dybantsa in the house, BYU blasts Fresno State
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - The highest rated recruit in BYU basketball history was at the Marriott Center Wednesday to watch his future team cruise to victory. AJ Dybantsa was witness to a 28-point drubbing by the Cougars over Fresno State, 95-67.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Lawmaker wants ‘flag planting’ at OSU to be a felony
item.description: Days after the postgame melee, a lawmaker introduced a bill that would make “flag planting” within Ohio Stadium a felony.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, NewsNation

item.title: Girls Basketball: Clairemont 24, Preuss 18
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- Girls basketball in Clairemont with Preuss dribbling against the Chieftains. A low scoring defensive game went the way for the home team with Clairemont winning 24-18.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: Robbie Gould announced as head football coach at Saint Viator High School
item.description: Former Chicago Bears kicker and franchise great Robbie Gould has been named the new head football coach at Saint Viator High School in Arlington Heights, and will begin his role immediately.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: SJ Earthquakes welcome 3 new players in deadline trade
item.description: (KRON) – Three players have been traded to the San Jose Earthquakes, the team announced Monday. Defender Dave Romney and midfielders Ian Harkes and Mark-Anthony Kaye will be making their way to Silicon Valley from the New England Revolution.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, KRON4
